sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 77

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 77:1
items.html:
(to the question) Nekeva [female]
Malchut of Atzilut is called by the name Nekeva, because she receives her Lights from ZA through the hole [Nekev] of the chest — see Answer 18 — for Lights that pass through a Masach and a hole are weakened and diminished; see Item 3. And there is a second reason: it is the initials of her four times, which are: Na'arah [maiden], Ketana [little one], Bogeret [mature one], and the lower Hey. (p. 759, Item 39; and p. 761, Item 44)
